is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study genotypes Plasmodium falciparum parasitaemias using glurp, msp1 and msp2 loci.

reports_target_markers.evidence: The paper only genotypes msp1, msp2 and glurp for distinguishing recrudescence from new infection; no drug-resistance codon markers (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfdhps, Pfk13, Pfmdr1, Pfcytb) are examined.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Only frequencies of new vs recrudescent infections are given (Table 1), not per-locus drug-resistance codon frequencies.

sub_country_location.evidence: Single site: Mbarara District Hospital, western Uganda.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Trial detailed in Priotto et al. 2003; a single-site short trial, though collection dates not explicitly given here.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical isolates from children in Mbarara, Uganda.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples come from the Priotto et al. 2003 Ugandan trial, part of a series of WHO/TDR coordinated combination therapy trials; a general report was to be published separately.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary drug-resistance marker data indicated; the study simply does not measure resistance markers.

assessment.summary: This is a molecular genotyping study distinguishing recrudescence from new infections in a P. falciparum treatment trial in Mbarara, Uganda, using msp1, msp2 and glurp. It does not examine any curated drug-resistance codon markers (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfdhps, Pfk13, Pfmdr1, Pfcytb) and reports no resistance-marker frequencies, so it is ineligible.
exclusion_reasons: No target drug-resistance markers examined (only msp1/msp2/glurp genotyping)
No extractable per-locus drug-resistance codon frequencies
